title: ECOGEO 'Omics Training: Amplicon Analysis with Mothur
author: Ben Tully
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.fi4bkgw

steps.title: Launch Mothur Software
steps.instruction: Move to the directory /home/c-debi/ecogeo/mothurdir and launch mothur.

steps.title: Setup for Rarefaction Analysis
steps.instruction: Open a second terminal window and navigate to /mothurdir to prepare for rarefaction analysis. Less output file.

steps.title: Create Rarefaction Curve Data File
steps.instruction: Create a file containing data for the rarefaction curve.

steps.title: Calculate Alpha Diversity
steps.instruction: Calculate alpha diversity measures in mothur using the command: summary.single(shared=stability.an.shared, calc=nseqs-coverage-sobs-invsimpson, subsample=2441). Then view the summary file: less stability.an.groups.ave-std.summary. If the command does not start with a '$', execute it within mothur.

steps.title: Examine Taxonomy and Relative Abundance
steps.instruction: Review the taxonomy using less stability.an.cons.taxonomy and relative abundance using less stability.an.0.03.subsample.shared.

steps.title: Prepare for Beta Diversity
steps.instruction: Begin the process for beta diversity measures.

steps.title: Generate and View Dendrogram
steps.instruction: Convert the distance measure output into a dendrogram using mothur with the command: tree.shared(phylip=stability.an.thetayc.0.03.lt.ave.dist). Quit mothur. View the generated dendrogram file: less stability.an.thetayc.0.03.lt.ave.tre. Copy the content before launching Dendroscope.
